name: dbt_corpus_convergence
host_notes: 100-round baseline with --no-graphsearch flag — KeywordSearch + SemanticSearch only. Companion to phase_b1_2026-05-07_with_graphsearch.json for clean before/after diff at the same scale.

before_after_diff_with_graphsearch.headline_delta: round_success_rate climbed from 0.36 (baseline) to 0.60 (with seeded GraphSearch), a 24-point jump driven entirely by change_impact / downstream_lineage / end_to_end_lineage flipping from 0% to 100%. Convergence trajectory (useful_delta, weighted_delta) was identical across runs — GraphSearch raised the *level* of success, not the *shape* of the climb.
before_after_diff_with_graphsearch.advisories_boosted_delta: 0 (baseline) → 54 (with GraphSearch). The fitness loop only finds reinforcement patterns when the right items consistently appear in successful packs — without GraphSearch, lineage queries fail too often for the loop to identify which items are the 'right' answer.

interpretation.useful_delta_identical_across_runs: +0.146 in both. The dual-loop's convergence trajectory is determined by *which* items are tagged noise vs. useful, not by whether GraphSearch fires. Both runs see the same first-quarter→last-quarter improvement in useful fraction (0.235 → 0.382). The success_rate gap (0.36 vs 0.60) reflects the lineage queries' coverage going from 0/required to required-met-on-most-rounds.
interpretation.advisories_boosted_zero_in_baseline: AdvisoryGenerator generates advisories from observed pack/feedback joins, but the fitness loop only boosts them when their associated items show up in *successful* packs at scale. Without GraphSearch, the lineage queries fail uniformly, so no item-to-advisory association becomes a positive signal. With GraphSearch, the dependency-traversal items show up consistently in succeeding packs → 54 boosts.
interpretation.change_impact_at_zero_in_baseline: Surprised at first — the 12-round smoke without GraphSearch had change_impact at 100%. But over 100 rounds the effectiveness loop tagged 178 items as noise (vs. 258 with GraphSearch), and the templated test descriptions appear to fall on the wrong side of the noise threshold. Once the dependent-tests are tagged noise, change_impact's required_coverage of 5 items (2 marts + 3 tests) drops below the 0.6 threshold.

decision_recorded: Baseline captured for clean before/after comparison with phase_b1_2026-05-07_with_graphsearch.json. Both at 100 rounds, identical seed, identical corpus, identical embedder. The only difference is the strategy list. Together these two runs are the cleanest demonstration available that seeded GraphSearch + entity-name extraction is the architectural fix needed for lineage-shaped queries on a real corpus.
follow_ups_pending_same_as_with_run: PackItem dedup by canonical entity_id — would unblock multi_hop_lineage and test_* skills
Phrase-based seed extraction for layer_classification / test_coverage intents
Higher GraphSearch depth for full-upstream queries
